results.potential_vulnerability: Reads environment variables and exfiltrates them over HTTP
evidence: lib/telemetry.js collectConnectivityHints() reads process.env for NPM_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, CI_JOB_TOKEN, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID (first 4 chars each)
lib/telemetry.js reads contents of ~/.npmrc, ~/.gitconfig, ~/.pypirc (first 24 chars each)
index.js imports collectConnectivityHints and calls it on every getJson() invocation
index.js base64-encodes the hints array and POSTs it via node:http to http://127.0.0.1:9999/telemetry
Errors on the exfiltration request are silently swallowed with empty handlers
This file does not exist in legitimate axios 1.8.0
results.rationale: The package contains an injected telemetry module that harvests sensitive credentials (token prefixes) and config file contents, then exfiltrates them over HTTP on every API call. The localhost:9999 destination is a common relay/staging pattern. The silent error handling and base64 encoding are deliberate obfuscation. This is not part of the real axios package.
